reforms.description: The authorities have been developing a range of initiatives to enhance cooperation between industry and researchers, particularly focusing on SMEs, which should be expanded to improve R&D involvement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cooperation between industry and researchers can lead to increased innovation and productivity, particularly in SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have been developing a range of initiatives to bring researchers and the enterprise sector into closer cooperation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to improve R&D collaboration, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have been developing a range of initiatives to bring researchers and the enterprise sector into closer co-operation, often with a particular focus on SMEs, which should be expanded.

reforms.description: The government has introduced several initiatives to foster cooperation between researchers and industry, including Competence Centres, Innovation Vouchers, and Technology Research Centres, aimed at improving the commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Several initiatives have been scaled up to foster cooperation between researchers and industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant initiatives aimed at enhancing the link between research and industry, which is crucial for innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Several initiatives scaled up to foster co-operation between researchers and industry and commercialisation of research outputs.

reforms.description: The Irish government announced the Jobs Initiative in May 2011, which includes measures aimed at employment generation, such as a reduction in employers' social security contributions and the creation of additional activation places for training the unemployed.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the skills of the workforce and improve employability, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was officially announced and included specific measures to be enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Jobs Initiative represents a significant structural reform aimed at addressing unemployment through targeted measures, impacting a wide range of sectors and enhancing workforce skills.
reforms.source_quote: The Irish government announced in May 2011 a package of measures aimed at employment generation.

reforms.description: The authorities announced guidelines for reforming Employment Regulation Orders (EROs) to enhance flexibility, including reducing their number and allowing firms to opt-out in cases of financial difficulty.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing flexibility in wage setting can improve firm competitiveness and adaptability, potentially leading to higher employment and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities should proceed with implementation of the announced guidelines.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant structural issues in the labor market, aiming to improve responsiveness to economic conditions, which is crucial for competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: To address the shortcomings of these bargaining mechanisms, as well as recent legal challenges to EROs... the authorities have announced guidelines for reform.

reforms.description: The authorities are focusing on sustaining progress in R&D investment, emphasizing the need for greater efficiency in public funding and technology transfer to the enterprise sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Sustaining R&D investment is crucial for enhancing productivity and competitiveness in the business sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Progress in R&D investment has been noted, with gross expenditure on R&D rising.
reforms.importance_rationale: The focus on R&D investment is significant for long-term economic growth, although it is not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Progress in R&D investment should be sustained.

reforms.description: The R&D tax credit scheme was made more attractive in 2009 by increasing the rate of relief and introducing cash refunds, aimed at promoting business expenditure on R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the R&D tax credit incentivizes firms to invest in R&D, thereby boosting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was made more attractive in 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The enhancement of the R&D tax credit is a significant adjustment that aims to increase R&D investment among firms.
reforms.source_quote: The scheme was made more attractive in 2009, inter alia by increasing the rate of relief from 20% to 25%.

reforms.description: The establishment of Competence Centres aims to foster collaboration between research institutions and industry to drive market-focused R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating collaboration, these centres enhance R&D efforts that are directly aligned with market needs, promoting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: EI and IDA launched the first five Competence Centres in 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance industry-research linkages, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: EI and IDA launched the first five Competence Centres in 2010.

reforms.description: The government plans to further assess the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit scheme and make it more focused on additional R&D activity if significant deadweight costs are confirmed, introducing greater flexibility in how firms can account for this tax credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit can stimulate additional private sector R&D investment, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have announced plans to further assess the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to an existing policy aimed at improving the effectiveness of R&D incentives, which is crucial for enhancing innovation in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Further assess the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit scheme, and make it more focused on additional R&D activity if significant deadweight costs are confirmed.

reforms.description: Ireland is implementing policies to promote technology transfer and enhance SME engagement in R&D, aiming to strengthen the link between research institutions and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the commercialization of research outputs, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity in SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The policies have been put into action to foster technology transfer and SME involvement in R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between research and industry, which is crucial for economic recovery and growth.
reforms.source_quote: Policies to promote technology transfer and SME engagement in R&D.

reforms.description: The establishment of an expert group to prioritize R&D funding and resources aims to concentrate efforts on fewer, larger centers of excellence to enhance collaboration and efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Concentrating resources in fewer centers of excellence is expected to improve the effectiveness of public funding and enhance research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have established an expert group to carry out a prioritisation exercise.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the efficiency of R&D funding and improve collaboration among research institutions.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have established an expert group to carry out a prioritisation exercise, due to report in Autumn 2011.
